sourceMovie.title: Catch Me If You Can
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Catch Me If You Can (2002) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Hustle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: American Hustle (2013) belongs to the same conversation as Catch Me If You Can — both anchor their drama in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Changeling
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Catch Me If You Can's drama stayed with you, Changeling (2008) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The crime through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Highwaymen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Catch Me If You Can excels in drama, The Highwaymen (2019) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: I Love You Phillip Morris
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: I Love You Phillip Morris (2010) is the natural next step after Catch Me If You Can: same drama DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and crime elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Old Man & the Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Catch Me If You Can who respond to its drama will find The Old Man & the Gun (2018) immediately familiar. Both films share a crime sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Breach
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Catch Me If You Can fans keep landing on Breach (2007) — the drama is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Wrong Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Wrong Man draws from the same drama well as Catch Me If You Can, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1956 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Kajillionaire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Kajillionaire (2020) captures the drama appeal of Catch Me If You Can without simply imitating it. The crime streak running through both films is what puts Kajillionaire at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Hacker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Catch Me If You Can — that dark edge, the crime — translates cleanly into Hacker (2016). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Now You See Me 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Now You See Me 2 (2016) clearly understood what makes crime work — the same quality that keeps Catch Me If You Can rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after Catch Me If You Can?
faq.answer: Try American Hustle, Changeling, The Highwaymen—each shares DNA with Catch Me If You Can while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Catch Me If You Can?
faq.answer: Catch Me If You Can (2002) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Catch Me If You Can worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
